messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Venezuelan census models
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

just a few thoughts:

1) Consideration of growth directly instead of (or in addition to) 
population abundance seems to be a good idea. Thanks for this idea.

2) I agree with Hans, that the purpose of the model counts, i.e. 
description of data, statistical testing, mechanistic systems 
understanding or prediction.

3) For a statistical model comparison, the sum of squares alone is not 
enough and model complexity (number of parameters) needs to be taken 
into account.

Re-fitting the two-compartment models with 3 resp. 5 parameters with 
nlsLM from package minpack.lm, we get:


 > AIC(res3, res5)
      df      AIC
res3  4 385.5084
res5  6 384.0203

We see that res5, the model with 5 parameters performs (indeed and only) 
a little bit better.

However, validity of all the autonomous models applied so far is rather 
limited and estimated saturation levels remain pure speculation. 
Therefore, non-autonomous models are required that consider external 
(i.e. "environmental") factors.

Thomas P.


PS: Code can be provided in case of interest.

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.body_plain:
Dear list,

using deSolve (or another package): is it possible to model a dampened oscillator that is reset to new states at specific points?

Background: I want to model a system that tries to approach equilibrium, but is disturbed at single events. It would roughly look like following screenshot:
https://dl.dropbox.com/u/4472780/WP/oscReset.PNG

In this screen shot, events at t=70 and t=120 disturb the oscillator. In this screen shot I simply pasted together 3 independent plots; but if the disturbance is directly modeled it should somehow be reflected in the dx etc. (e.g., at the second event I would expect that the curve first continues going up before it is regulated down again).

Here's my function for the dampened oscillator:

library("deSolve")

	osci <- function (t, states, parms) {
	 with(as.list(c(states,  parms)), {
	   dx <- y
	   dy <- eta * x + zeta * y
	   list(c(dx, dy))
	 })
	}

	states <- c(x=1, y=0)
	out <- data.frame(ode(states, osci, times = 1:100, parms = c(eta = -0.05, zeta = -0.02)))
	plot(out)


Best,
Felix

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.body_plain:
Hi Felix,

With simecol, which builds on deSolve you could do the following (based on an example Thomas Petzoldt send me a couple of years ago):

library(simecol)

osci <- odeModel( main=function(time, init, parms,...){
  x <- init
  p <- parms
  ## same as above, but now we have and input, or external stimulus
  S <- approxTime1(inputs, time, rule=2)["s.in"]
  dx <- x[2]
  dy <- parms["eta"] * x[1] + parms["zeta"] * x[2] + S
  list(c(dx, dy))
}, 
                  times=c(from=0, to=200, by=0.2),
                  parms=c(eta=-0.05, zeta=-0.02, x=1, y=0),
                  initfunc = function(obj) {
                    init(obj)[c("x", "y")] <- parms(obj)[c("x", "y")]
                    return(obj)
                  }
)


## Let's give the system a kick at 70 and 120:
 
kick <- as.matrix(data.frame(time=1:200, 
                             s.in=c(rep(0,69), 
                                    1, 
                                    rep(0,49), 1, 
                                    rep(0,80))))
inputs(osci) <- kick

mySim <-  out(sim(osci))
plot(y ~ time, data=mySim, t="l")


I hope this helps.

Best regards

Markus

-----Original Message-----
From: r-sig-dynamic-models-bounces at r-project.org [mailto:r-sig-dynamic-models-bounces at r-project.org] On Behalf Of Felix Sch?nbrodt
Sent: 09 January 2013 14:33
To: r-sig-dynamic-models at r-project.org
Subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets

Dear list,

using deSolve (or another package): is it possible to model a dampened oscillator that is reset to new states at specific points?

Background: I want to model a system that tries to approach equilibrium, but is disturbed at single events. It would roughly look like following screenshot:
https://dl.dropbox.com/u/4472780/WP/oscReset.PNG

In this screen shot, events at t=70 and t=120 disturb the oscillator. In this screen shot I simply pasted together 3 independent plots; but if the disturbance is directly modeled it should somehow be reflected in the dx etc. (e.g., at the second event I would expect that the curve first continues going up before it is regulated down again).

Here's my function for the dampened oscillator:

library("deSolve")

	osci <- function (t, states, parms) {
	 with(as.list(c(states,  parms)), {
	   dx <- y
	   dy <- eta * x + zeta * y
	   list(c(dx, dy))
	 })
	}

	states <- c(x=1, y=0)
	out <- data.frame(ode(states, osci, times = 1:100, parms = c(eta = -0.05, zeta = -0.02)))
	plot(out)


Best,
Felix

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Modeling dampened oscillator with random resets
messages.body_plain:
On 09.01.2013 15:33, Felix Sch?nbrodt wrote:
> Dear list,
>
> using deSolve (or another package): is it possible to model a
> dampened oscillator that is reset to new states at specific points?

Yes, this is possible, using the event mechanism:


library(deSolve)
osci <- function (t, states, parms) {
   with(as.list(c(states,  parms)), {
     dx <- y
     dy <- eta * x + zeta * y
     list(c(dx, dy))
   })
}

states <- c(x = 1, y = 0)
out <- ode(states, osci, times = 1:200,
   parms = c(eta = -0.05, zeta = -0.02))
plot(out)


###  see ?events for details
eventdat <- data.frame(var = "y", time = c(70, 120),
                        value = c(0.7, 1), method = "add")

out <- ode(states, osci, times = 1:200,
   parms = c(eta = -0.05, zeta = -0.02),
   events = list(data = eventdat))
plot(out, which = "y")
abline(v = eventdat$time, col = "red", lty = "dashed")




## Thomas P.

messages.body_snippet:
Dear list, 

I am using deSolve to solve a set of ODEs and am trying to make use of the events mechanism. Is there a way to apply two different events (defined as functions) at different times?

Using

messages.body_snippet:
Dear Tom,

We are using the root finding function of deSolve to fire multiple events at specific times.
Simply add an extra ODE that starts at t0 and has a derivative value of '1'.
You can then use a

messages.body_snippet:

what about creating a dispatch function

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] deSolve events - different event functions at different times
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] deSolve events - different event functions at different times
messages.body_plain:
Thanks both for the suggestions. The root based method does exactly what I need.
 
Thanks again
 
Tom

>>> Thomas Petzoldt <Thomas.Petzoldt at tu-dresden.de> 25/01/2013 17:33 >>>
On 25.01.2013 16:27, Faelens Ruben wrote:

> @List: I am very open to alternative suggestions that depend less on
> the precision of the solver though...

Hi,

what about creating a dispatch function that decides between the two (or
more) events?

Have fun!

Thomas P.

##-- two events ---------------------------------------

library("deSolve")

## Derivative function
derivs <- function(t, v, parms) list(c(0, -0.5 * v[2]))

## events
event1 <- function(t, y, parms){
   cat("event1 at t=", t, "\n")
   with (as.list(y), {
	 v1 <- v1 + 1
	 v2 <- 5 * runif(1)
	 return(c(v1, v2))
   })
}

event2 <- function(t, y, parms){
   cat("event2 at t=", t, "\n")
   with (as.list(y), {
	 v1 <- v1 + 0.5 * v2
	 v2 <- 5 * runif(1)
	 return(c(v1, v2))
   })
}

etimes1 <- c(1, 3, 4, 5)
etimes2 <- c(2, 4, 6)
allevents <- sort(unique(c(etimes1, etimes2)))

dispatch <- function(t, y, parms) {
   ## important! conflict resolution
   ##   what if 2 events occur at the same time?
   ## here: use 2nd event
   ##   alternatives: add, average, randomize, ...
   ret <- y
   if (t %in% etimes1) ret <- event1(t, y, parms)
   if (t %in% etimes2) ret <- event2(t, y, parms)
   return(ret)
}

out <- ode(func = derivs,
   y = c(v1  = 1, v2 = 2),
   times = seq(0, 10, by=0.1),
   parms = NULL,
   events = list(func = dispatch, time = allevents))

plot(out)
abline(v=etimes1, col="blue", lty="dashed")
abline(v=etimes2, col="red", lty="dotted", lwd=2)

_______________________________________________
R-sig-dynamic-models mailing list
R-sig-dynamic-models at r-project.org
https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-dynamic-models
